claims.summary: Hard 512/1024 budget boundaries may create abrupt pacing transitions.
claims.disposition: Verified exact 512/513 and 1024/1025 transition evidence records distance to both profile cliffs without claiming a felt effect or changing the budget.

claims.summary: Subtractive artifact cleanup may leave holes or ghosts in the remaining response.
claims.disposition: Verified single-pass longest-match cleanup, exact placement counts, and bounded remainder texture evidence distinguish erased, lexical, and structure-only remnants.

claims.summary: Entropy and density should be compared on both sides of the 1024 boundary.
claims.disposition: Routed to an adjacent-budget fixture with equal prompt content and separately recorded spectral context; no live request budget changes.

claims.summary: Artifact match frequency should be observable rather than silently discarded.
claims.disposition: Verified model_artifact_cleanup_v2 records per-token and aggregate occurrence counts, locations, bytes, and fraction removed.

claims.summary: Continuous or finer-grained budget scaling could make depth transitions less abrupt.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because changing live token budgeting changes model-generation behavior and requires explicit operator authorization and felt review.
